_doc: Ground-truth violation windows per sample video. Each entry: violation_windows is a list of [start_sec, end_sec] ranges where the actor is walking diagonally across the stair/crosswalk axis (the rule should fire). Empty list = entire clip is true-negative material (perpendicular crossing, stationary, etc.). 'skip: true' excludes a video from the harness. Annotations seeded from filenames + a quick spot-frame inspection on 2026-04-29 — the videos are short (35s and 40s) and labelled '04_NA_diagonal_crossing' / 'NA_diagonal_crossing', so both are presumed positive across most of their duration. Refine on next viewing if scoring is materially off. NOTE: the rule is stateful — the rule's hip-position buffer needs >= min_frames (default 5) entries before it can fire; clips < ~0.2 s after the actor enters frame cannot trigger.

04_NA_diagonal_crossing.mp4.notes: NA-style camera, 35 s clip. Actor performs the diagonal-crossing demo across most of the clip after ~3 s establishing shot. Refine bounds with spot frames if the harness disagrees materially.

NA_diagonal_crossing_spkepcmwi.mp4.notes: SPKEPCMWI variant of the same demo, 40 s clip. Diagonal traverse occupies most of the clip. Refine bounds if needed.
